title: Areas Related to Circles
summary: Chapter 12 calculates areas and perimeters related to circles, sectors, and segments. Students learn formulas for circumference and area of a circle, arc length and area of a sector, and area of a segment. These are applied to find areas of combinations of plane figures involving circles, triangles, squares, and rectangles.
learningPoints: Recall circumference = 2πr and area = πr² of a circle
Define sector as region between two radii and an arc
Define segment as region between chord and arc
Calculate area of minor segment = area of sector - area of triangle
Calculate area of major segment = area of circle - area of minor segment
Solve problems involving combinations of circles with squares, rectangles and triangles
Find areas of figures with cut-outs and shaded regions
Apply correct value of π (22/7 or 3.14) as specified

formulas.label: Circumference of Circle
formulas.description: Perimeter of a complete circle with radius r.

formulas.label: Area of Circle
formulas.description: Area enclosed by a circle of radius r.

formulas.label: Arc Length
formulas.description: Length of arc of a sector with central angle θ and radius r.

formulas.label: Area of Sector
formulas.description: Area of sector with central angle θ and radius r.

formulas.label: Area of Minor Segment
formulas.expression: A = Area of sector - Area of triangle OAB
formulas.description: Area between chord AB and minor arc.

formulas.label: Area of Triangle in Sector
formulas.description: Area of triangle formed by two radii and chord when central angle is θ.

resources.label: Areas Related to Circles — Video Lessons
resources.description: Videos on sector, segment areas and combination figures.

resources.label: NCERT Mathematics Textbook — Class 10
resources.description: Official NCERT textbook Chapter 12 Areas Related to Circles.

mcqs.question: Area of a circle with diameter 14cm is:
Arc length of sector with radius 7cm and angle 90° is:
Area of sector with radius 12cm and angle 60° is:
Circumference of a circle is 44cm. Its area is:
A sector has area 77cm² and radius 7cm. The angle is:
Area of minor segment when radius=10 and angle=90° is (use π=3.14):
mcqs.explanation: Area of sector = (90/360)×3.14×100 = 78.5. Area of triangle = ½×10×10 = 50. Segment = 78.5-50 = 28.5 cm².

mcqs.question: A horse is tied to a corner of square field of side 14m with rope 7m. Area it can graze:
Area of ring between two concentric circles of radii 8 and 5 is:
Perimeter of sector with radius 10.5 and angle 60° is:
Four equal circles each of radius 7cm are arranged touching each other. Area enclosed between them:
mcqs.explanation: Square formed: side=14cm, area=196. 4 quarter circles = 1 full circle = 154cm². Enclosed = 196-154 = 42 cm².

mcqs.question: Area of equilateral triangle inscribed in circle of radius 6cm is:
mcqs.explanation: Side of equilateral triangle = r√3 = 6√3. Area = (√3/4)(6√3)² = (√3/4)×108 = 27√3 cm².

mcqs.question: If radius is doubled, area of circle becomes:
mcqs.explanation: Area = πr². If r→2r, area = π(2r)² = 4πr². 4 times the original.

mcqs.question: Area of major segment = area of circle minus:
mcqs.explanation: Major segment = Total circle area - Minor segment area.

mcqs.question: A circle is inscribed in a square of side 14cm. Area of shaded region outside circle:
mcqs.explanation: Circle radius = 7cm. Area of square = 196. Area of circle = 154. Shaded = 196-154 = 42 cm².

mcqs.question: Ratio of areas of sector with angle 90° to full circle is:
mcqs.explanation: 90°/360° = 1/4. Sector area is 1/4 of full circle.

exercises.question: Find area of sector of circle with radius 6cm and angle 60°.
Find the area of minor segment of circle radius 14cm with central angle 60°.
exercises.answer: Area of sector = (60/360)×22/7×196 = 102.67 cm². Triangle: equilateral with side 14cm. Area = (√3/4)×196 = 84.87 cm². Segment = 102.67 - 84.87 = 17.8 cm².

exercises.question: In a circle of radius 21cm, an arc subtends 60° at centre. Find arc length and area of sector.
A brooch is made with silver wire in shape of circle of diameter 35mm. A wire is used for 5 diameters. Find length of wire.
exercises.answer: Circumference = 22/7 × 35 = 110 mm. 5 diameters = 5 × 35 = 175 mm. Total = 110 + 175 = 285 mm.

exercises.question: A chord of circle of radius 15cm subtends 60° at centre. Find areas of minor and major segments.
exercises.answer: Minor sector = (60/360)×π×225 = 37.5π = 117.86 cm². Triangle area = ½×15×15×sin60° = 97.31 cm². Minor segment = 20.55 cm². Major segment = π×225 - 20.55 = 686.9 cm².

exercises.question: Four circular cardboard pieces each of radius 7cm are placed on a table touching each other. Find area of gap between them.
exercises.answer: Square formed by centres has side 14cm. Area of square = 196 cm². 4 quarter circles = 1 full circle of radius 7 = 154 cm². Gap = 196 - 154 = 42 cm².

exercises.question: In equilateral triangle of side 12cm, three arcs are drawn each with radius 6cm at each vertex. Find area of remaining triangle.
exercises.answer: Area of triangle = (√3/4)×144 = 36√3 = 62.35 cm². Each arc is 60° sector. 3 sectors = ½ circle = ½×π×36 = 56.57 cm². Remaining = 62.35 - 56.57 = 5.78 cm².

exercises.question: A circular park of radius 20m has a path 5m wide around it. Find area of path.
exercises.answer: Inner radius = 20m. Outer radius = 25m. Area of path = π(25²-20²) = π×225 = 706.86 m².
